insight: Eviscerator at 9BR is considered too expensive for macro use, making it nearly niche/rush-only, whereas at 8BR it was considered busted. masn6811 argues that a 1-gold nerf can swing a unit from broken to nearly unusable, citing this as a cautionary example for nerfing other units.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion about how to nerf Felid/Twinbolt, with eviscerator used as a precedent for overcorrection via 1-gold nerfs.

insight: Felid (Twinbolt) at 6RR EEE is considered above the curve but not autowin-inducing; masn6811 argues it is skill-testing, beatable by going over it, and does not force specific build orders the way old Thorium Dynamo or Centurion did. It differs from units like old Smorcus/Fission Turret which led to autowins and cutthroat rushes.
author: masn6811
context: Debate over how much to nerf Felid/Twinbolt, with masn arguing its current form does not create degenerate gameplay.

insight: xyotsuba argues that Twinbolt/Felid at 7RR EEE is still above the curve because it matches Bombarder efficiency while requiring only 1 tech building instead of 3, making a 1-gold nerf less punishing than for other units. masn6811 counters that at 7RR the unit becomes overshadowed whenever Electrovore is in the set, since Electrovore is a superior energy sink.
author: consensus
context: Detailed cost-comparison debate over Twinbolt nerf options, referencing efficiency benchmarks.

insight: masn6811 argues that part of Felid/Twinbolt's value is mid-game Engineer cycling, which requires an economy to execute, and that its 2HP body after firing is weak if the opponent plays defense and builds a bigger absorber. This means very fast rushes with Felid are not reliably winning because the natural second-player economy advantage enables better mid-game Engineer cycling with BA tech.
author: masn6811
context: masn explaining why Felid does not lead to autowin fastimus rushes, as part of the nerf debate.

insight: elyot states that Apollo and Savior are intentionally kept weak by design because they are annoying and game-warping when dominant, reflecting a design philosophy of deliberately keeping certain high-impact units below the efficiency curve.
author: elyot
context: cooledcannon quoted elyot's earlier statement about Apollo being weak by design, in the context of Apollo gameplay discussion.

insight: Apollo has diminishing returns when multiple copies are purchased and is countered by Gauss Cannon ('geese'); masn6811 cites games where going 3-4 Apollos was fun and felt fair even when losing, pointing to its push-pull decision of wanting Apollo but needing to know when to stop.
author: masn6811
context: masn sharing replay examples of multi-Apollo games as evidence of balanced, interactive gameplay.

insight: Eviscerator at 9BR has very little macro use and is primarily a rush tool only when the opponent reaches 5 or more units; masn6811 requests a revert to 7BR, arguing the current version is 'completely unusable' in the midgame. awaclus agrees it is underwhelming, though elyot finds the 'completely unusable' framing hard to believe.
author: masn6811
context: Short thread where masn requests an eviscerator hotfix to 7BR for an upcoming brawl event.

insight: masn6811 explains that the Frontline keyword is primarily a Blue color-pie trait not because of the mechanic itself, but because Frontline is barely a drawback on non-Blue units since those units have little absorb to deny anyway. On a Green unit like Barrier, Frontline would serve a meaningful purpose by denying perfect granularity, which is a core Green theme.
author: masn6811
context: Debate over whether to give Barrier the Frontline keyword as a balance/design change.

insight: Valk's primary strategic value is holding attack to deny Barrier absorb while simultaneously threatening to snipe Barriers for exploit value; this 'hold to deny' pattern is described as a massive part of the unit's value, and making Barrier Frontline would fundamentally alter this gameplay interaction, essentially creating a different unit.
author: xyotsuba
context: Discussion of whether making Barrier Frontline would break Valk's design, with silentslayers and xyotsuba both agreeing on Valk's absorb-denial pattern.

insight: In a base set + Valk asymmetric scenario (base set viability test), the P1 opening line is: T1 DD, T2 DD, T3 DDC, T4 DDB, T5 WDDA, T6 ValkT. P2 is forced into Fastimus. silentslayers notes Valk is base-set viable and that its absorb efficiency when soaked makes it stronger than its tri-color cost suggests.
author: silentslayers
context: megasupp was surprised a tri-color attacker was base-set viable; silentslayers provided the concrete opening line.

insight: Current Galvani Drone is considered so efficient that it is almost always wrong to kill it (opponents should instead target Engineers to deny energy); its Frontline status exists primarily to allow exploit scenarios, and a rework to remove Frontline is desired by several community members to address the problem where Frontline prevents using Galvani for absorb when being rushed.
author: consensus
context: Extended thread discussing a proposed Galvani Drone rework (Mega Galvani) and the problems with its current Frontline design.

insight: A proposed Mega Galvani redesign (4EE cost, 4 supply, 5HP, EEE->3 Drones ability) is debated: at 3HP it would be as efficient as current Galvani (which the community considers too efficient), so 5HP is preferred to push it above the absorb threshold. At 5HP, sniping it to deny 2 absorb is still correct in many cases and is not backbreaking, while at 4HP exploit opportunities exist that make it too killable.
author: consensus
context: Detailed HP and Frontline tradeoff analysis for the proposed Mega Galvani rework.

insight: 'Base set viable' is a specific test in the Prismata community referring to a scenario where P2 is forced into Fastimus and P1 has exclusive access to the unit being tested; it is used to evaluate whether a unit is strong enough to justify buying it over base-set options from a first-player perspective.
author: silentslayers
context: silentslayers clarifying the meaning of 'base set viable' to megasupp who was surprised by Valk's viability rating.

insight: masn6811 ranks Cryo Ray variants by power level as: Gauss Charge pack > 3HP Cryo Ray > LS5 Cryo Ray > LS4 Cryo Ray, suggesting the lifespan versions are significantly weaker than a persistent 3HP version, with LS4 being substantially worse than LS5.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion about which version of Cryo Ray to keep after balance testing, with community members expressing displeasure with the new lifespan version.

insight: p0lari notes that the lifespan version of Cryo Ray most severely hurts units like Plexo Cell, Barrier, and Asteri (rather than D-Grid), because those units rely on persistent absorb granularity that lifespan Cryo Ray can exploit by freezing and destroying in sequence. D-Grid requires committing to 7 freeze to get big value and only works once.
author: p0lari
context: Cryo Ray redesign discussion focusing on which defensive units are most hurt by the lifespan version.

insight: elyot states that Twinbolt/Felid at 7RR EEE would be acceptable at that cost, but warns it will punish weaker players who buy it without knowing how to use it well, suggesting the unit has a high skill floor that makes it deceptively difficult to evaluate purely from win rates.
author: elyot
context: elyot weighing in at the end of the extended Felid nerf debate.

insight: elyot confirms barrier is extremely unlikely to receive a Frontline change, and notes that a '1HP frontline defender' design feels Blue in color pie terms, not Green, reinforcing the existing color-pie framework.
author: elyot
context: elyot responding to the barrier Frontline debate after the community requested his input.
